question: What is the primary mechanism by which CYP3A4 enzyme induction by rifampin reduces the efficacy of co-administered drugs?
options: Rifampin directly binds and inactivates the co-administered drug
Rifampin increases CYP3A4 expression, accelerating drug metabolism and reducing plasma concentrations
Rifampin competitively inhibits intestinal absorption of the co-administered drug
Rifampin increases renal clearance of the co-administered drug
explanation: Rifampin is a potent CYP3A4 inducer via PXR activation. Increased CYP3A4 expression accelerates hepatic and intestinal first-pass metabolism of co-administered drugs, reducing their bioavailability and efficacy.
